zh.lifeSummary: 當Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant於1188年9月10日出生在法國洛萊姆斯布拉班吉素-斯時，他的父親Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen28歲，母親Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace26歲。他於1215年8月8日與Maria von Staufen在法國洛萊姆斯布拉班吉素-斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1235年被列為hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248)。他去世於1248年2月1日在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文享年59歲，埋葬在比利時布拉班特 瓦隆維勒斯-拉-維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant于1188年9月10日出生在法国洛萊姆斯布拉班吉素-斯时，他的父亲Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen28岁，母亲Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace26岁。他于1215年8月8日与Maria von Staufen在法国洛萊姆斯布拉班吉素-斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1235年被列为hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248)。他去世于1248年2月1日在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文享年59岁，埋葬在比利时布拉班特 瓦隆維勒斯-拉-維爾。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Hendrik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Henrik .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: 'de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant was born on 10 September 1188, in Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, France, his father, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, was 28 and his mother, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, was 26. He married Maria von Staufen on 8 August 1215, in Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. In 1235, his occupation is listed as hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248). He died on 1 February 1248, in Leuven, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, at the age of 59, and was buried in Villers-la-Ville, Walloon Brabant, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant est né le 10 septembre 1188, à Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, France, son père, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Maria von Staufen le 8 août 1215, à Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. En 1235, sa profession de son indiquée est hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248). Il est décédé le 1 février 1248, à Leuven, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Villers-la-Ville, Brabant wallon, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant am 10. September 1188 in Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen 28 und seine Mutter Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace 26. Er hat am 8. August 1215 in Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich Maria von Staufen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1235 ist sein Beruf als hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248) aufgeführt. Er starb am 1. Februar 1248 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Leuven, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien und wurde in Villers-la-Ville, Wallonisch Brabant, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant è nato il il 10 settembre 1188 nel Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, il suo padre, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Maria von Staufen il 8 agosto 1215, nel Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1235, la sua occupazione è indicata come hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248). Morì il 1 febbraio 1248, nel Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Villers-la-Ville, Brabant wallon, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabantが1188年9月10日にフランスロレィヌメウゼブラバント-スル-メウゼで生まれた時，父Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingenは28歳，母Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsaceは26歳であった。1215年8月8日にフランスロレィヌメウゼブラバント-スル-メウゼでMaria von Staufenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1235年，職業はhertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248)と記録されている。彼は1248年2月1日にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントレウベンで59歳で死去し，ベルギーブラバント ワロンヴィラース-ラ-ヴィルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant(이)가 1188년 9월 10일 프랑스 로레이인 우스 브라반트-수-우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1215년 8월 8일 프랑스 로레이인 우스 브라반트-수-우스에서 Maria von Staufen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1235년, 그의 직업은 hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248)이었다. 그는 1248년 2월 1일 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 레우븐에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 브라반트 월런 비레르스-라-빌에 묻혔다.

mn.10. September 1188–1 February 1248: 1188 оны Есдүгээр сарын 10–1248 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 1

pt.10. September 1188–1 February 1248: 10 de setembro de 1188–1 de fevereiro de 1248
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant nasceu em 10 de setembro de 1188, em Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, França, seu pai, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Maria von Staufen em 8 de agosto de 1215, em Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1235, sua profissão aparece como hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248). Ele faleceu em 1 de fevereiro de 1248, em Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Villers-la-Ville, Brabant wallon, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant родился 10 сентября 1188 г. в местности брабант-сур-меус, меус, лорреаин, Франция, его отцу по имени Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria von Staufen 8 августа 1215 г. в местности брабант-сур-меус, меус, лорреаин, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. В 1235 году он работал по профессии hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248). Он умер 1 февраля 1248 г. в местности леувен, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности виллирс-ла-вилл, брабант валлон, Бельгия.

es.10. September 1188–1 February 1248: 10 de septiembre de 1188–1 de febrero de 1248
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant nació el 10 de septiembre de 1188, en Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, su papá, Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Maria von Staufen el 8 de agosto de 1215, en Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1235, su ocupación registrada era hertog van brabant, duke of brabant, (1235-1248). Murió el 1 de febrero de 1248, en Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Villers-la-Ville, Brabant wallon, Bélgica.
